title: stephanomeria guadalupensis
intro: Stephanomeria guadalupensis, also known as Guadalupe wirelettuce, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Stephanomeria guadalupensis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying and steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
